mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and enhances security for agent-based applications, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential resource overhead for running AI agents.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying the Helm chart.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace, ensuring all necessary components are deployed.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> This installs Kagenti with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Kagenti, which defines the configuration for an AI agent.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

```

> Replace the image with the desired agent image.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagent example-agent -o yaml

```

> Look for 'status' to confirm the agent is running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the agents and may leave orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command removes the Helm release.
> This command deletes the namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version while ensuring the existing resources are preserved.

```bash

kubectl get kagent -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagent example-agent -o yaml

```

> Backup existing Custom Resources.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify the Custom Resource status post-upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KAgent resource is created with the correct name and namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure it is available in the specified container registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KAgent container is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the KAgent pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your Kubernetes cluster status and network connectivity.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent resource with a status indicating it is running and healthy. Example output: 'status: { phase: Running }'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: kagenti/agent:latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
